features: TL-UL CSR read/write access with byte-addressed 32-bit registers
AES-128 ECB and CBC mode control via CSRs
AES encrypt and decrypt operation sequencing
128-bit data input programming across four 32-bit writes
128-bit data output readback across four 32-bit reads
Start trigger handling and operation completion interrupt
Status polling for output_valid and input_ready behavior
Reset behavior and CSR default state
CBC IV update across multi-block sequences
TL-UL protocol compliance under ready/valid handshakes
Alert output observation for fatal error conditions

directed_tests.name: reset_smoke_and_csr_sanity
directed_tests.intent: Apply reset, verify TL-UL accessibility, confirm CSR defaults, and check that status/interrupt/alert outputs are quiescent after reset.

directed_tests.name: ecb_encrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program a known AES-128 key and plaintext in ECB mode, trigger encryption, poll for completion, and compare ciphertext against a golden reference.

directed_tests.name: ecb_decrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program a known AES-128 key and ciphertext in ECB mode, trigger decryption, and verify recovered plaintext matches the golden reference.

directed_tests.name: cbc_encrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program key, IV, and plaintext for CBC encryption, trigger operation, and verify ciphertext plus IV update behavior against a golden model.

directed_tests.name: cbc_decrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program key, IV, and ciphertext for CBC decryption, trigger operation, and verify plaintext plus IV update behavior against a golden model.

directed_tests.name: back_to_back_ecb_blocks
directed_tests.intent: Issue consecutive ECB start triggers with new input data as soon as input_ready allows, verifying output retention, throughput behavior, and no data corruption between blocks.

directed_tests.name: clear_trigger_behavior
directed_tests.intent: Exercise key_iv_data_in_clear and data_out_clear separately, confirming that programmed registers are zeroized and that output clearing does not disturb unrelated control state.

directed_tests.name: interrupt_completion_flow
directed_tests.intent: Enable interrupt generation if supported by CSR map, start an operation, and verify intr_o asserts on completion and deasserts according to expected acknowledge/clear behavior.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzz_smoke
random_tests.intent: Run constrained-random TL-UL reads and writes over the discovered CSR space, checking protocol stability, legal access behavior, and absence of bus hangs within the time budget.

random_tests.name: mode_operation_random_matrix
random_tests.intent: Randomize ECB/CBC, encrypt/decrypt, key/data values, and start timing across a small set of transactions, comparing results to a software AES reference model.

random_tests.name: back_to_back_random_stream
random_tests.intent: Generate a short stream of random blocks with randomized inter-block gaps to stress input_ready, DONE-to-IDLE transitions, and output overwrite hazards.

random_tests.name: reset_during_idle_and_busy
random_tests.intent: Assert reset at random points during idle and active operation to validate safe recovery, CSR reinitialization, and no stuck interrupt or alert conditions.

risk_areas.area: AES algorithm correctness in ECB/CBC encrypt and decrypt paths
risk_areas.reason: Core functionality is the highest-value risk; any mismatch against FIPS-197 behavior breaks the block cipher engine.
Implementation-dependent key schedule timing and caching can introduce subtle state bugs across consecutive operations.

risk_areas.area: CBC IV handling and multi-block chaining
risk_areas.reason: IV update semantics are easy to implement incorrectly and directly affect chained ciphertext/plaintext correctness.
DONE/input_ready transitions and output overwrite behavior are explicitly supported and likely to expose FSM timing issues.

risk_areas.area: TL-UL CSR access ordering and partial 128-bit register assembly
risk_areas.reason: The design relies on four-word programming for key, IV, and data; word ordering or write acceptance bugs are common integration failures.

risk_areas.area: Trigger bit interactions with clear operations
risk_areas.reason: The spec notes undefined behavior when start and clear are asserted together, so the implementation may have corner-case hazards.
Completion interrupt and fatal alert outputs are externally visible and must not glitch or remain stuck.
Asynchronous reset can expose FSM and CSR initialization issues, especially with active bus traffic.
Within a 60-minute autonomous budget, exhaustive bus stress is not feasible, so only a bounded smoke/fuzz pass is practical.
